On initial load, the page reads the section query parameter from the URL (e.g., dashboard.html?section=assessments). If the value matches one of the known sections ('assessments', 'plans', or 'worksheets'), that section is set as the active tab in the dashboard state. If section is missing or does not match a known section, the page falls back to a default section (for example, 'assessments'). The section state determines which list component (assessments, plans, or worksheets) is visible, while others are hidden but still hold their data in memory.

Once the initial section state is determined, the page calls getDashboardOverview a single time to populate the dashboard data: savedAssessments (array of AssessmentInstance), savedPlans (array of CustomPlan), savedWorksheets (array of objects combining SavedWorksheet and Worksheet), and stats. A top-level loading state is active until this call resolves or fails. On success, the data is stored in in-memory state and passed down to child components. On failure, an error state is stored and rendered.

When the user clicks a tab to change sections, the active section state is updated and the browser URL is updated to include or replace the section query parameter with the new value. This ensures that refreshing the page or sharing the URL will reopen the dashboard with the same active section. This tab switch does not trigger additional calls to getDashboardOverview because all data is already in memory.

The quick preview for assessments maintains its own in-memory state: selectedAssessmentInstanceId, previewLoading, previewError, and previewData. When a user triggers a quick preview, the page calls getAssessmentResultDetail with the selected assessmentInstanceId. While waiting, previewLoading is true. Upon success, previewData is set to the returned object containing instance (AssessmentInstance) and assessment (Assessment), and previewLoading is set to false. On error, previewError is set and previewLoading is false. This preview state is not encoded in the URL; it is purely in-memory to keep the URL surface limited to section control and avoid undocumented parameters.

No localStorage or other persistent client storage is used to manage dashboard state. All persisted information comes from the server via getDashboardOverview and getAssessmentResultDetail, and any state transitions are reflected in the URL only through the section query parameter. Navigations to assessment_detail.html, custom_plan.html, and worksheet_detail.html use their respective URL parameters (instanceId, planId, id) but those target pages manage their own data loading independently.

components.functionality: Initializes the dashboard, parses URL parameters, orchestrates calls to getDashboardOverview, manages global loading and error states, tracks the active section, and coordinates data flow to all child components.
Displays and manages the section controls for 'Assessments', 'Plans', and 'Worksheets'. Reflects and updates the active section based on the section URL parameter and user clicks.
Shows an at-a-glance summary of the counts of saved assessments, plans, and worksheets using the stats object returned by getDashboardOverview. Automatically updates if the overview data is reloaded.
Container for all assessment-related UI, including the saved-assessments list and the quick preview panel. Only visible when the active section is set to 'assessments'.

Displays the list of saved assessment instances returned in savedAssessments. Each item shows key fields available on AssessmentInstance (such as completed_at, score, result_summary) and provides actions to open the full detail page or to load a quick preview.

Shows a quick preview of a selected saved assessment by calling getAssessmentResultDetail with an assessmentInstanceId. Displays data from both the AssessmentInstance and its Assessment definition when available.
Container for the list of saved custom plans. Only visible when the active section is set to 'plans'.

Displays the list of CustomPlan items returned in savedPlans. Each entry shows plan information derived from CustomPlan (such as name, total_cost, timestamps) and allows navigation to custom_plan.html for full details.
Container for the list of saved worksheets/resources. Only visible when the active section is set to 'worksheets'.

Displays the list of saved worksheet entries, each combining a SavedWorksheet record with its associated Worksheet entity. Shows Worksheet metadata such as title, topic, format, and page_count, and provides navigation to worksheet_detail.html.

Shows a generic, section-aware empty state when the currently active section has no items. The content is determined by checking whether the corresponding list (savedAssessments, savedPlans, or savedWorksheets) is empty, without using hardcoded example data.
Displays a global loading indicator while getDashboardOverview is in progress, and hides it once data has loaded or an error occurs.
Displays an error message and retry control when getDashboardOverview fails. Replaces the main content area until the request is successfully retried.
Displays an inline error message and retry option within the assessment-preview-panel when getAssessmentResultDetail fails.
Ensures that changes to the active section are reflected in the section URL parameter and listens for initial URL to set the starting active section state.

layout.grid_system: Implied 12-column responsive grid with a centered maximum content width (likely 1140–1280px). Hero content appears to span about 6–7 columns for text and 5–6 columns for the image.
layout.layout_pattern: The dashboard adopts the mockup’s linear top-to-bottom structure with a clear hero-like top band inside the main content area. Immediately below the global site header, a full-width dashboard strip contains the section tabs at the top-left (analogous to the small category pill), followed by a large summary stats block on the left (analogous to the hero heading) and a vertical detail panel on the right (analogous to the hero image). Below this hero band, the page continues in a single main content zone split into a wider left column and a narrower right column: the left column stacks the three content sections (assessments, plans, worksheets) vertically, while the right column continues to host the persistent preview panel aligned with the top content. All content sits within a centered max-width container, following a 12-column grid.

layout_strategies.content_arrangement: The dashboard adopts the mockup’s linear top-to-bottom structure with a clear hero-like top band inside the main content area. Immediately below the global site header, a full-width dashboard strip contains the section tabs at the top-left (analogous to the small category pill), followed by a large summary stats block on the left (analogous to the hero heading) and a vertical detail panel on the right (analogous to the hero image). Below this hero band, the page continues in a single main content zone split into a wider left column and a narrower right column: the left column stacks the three content sections (assessments, plans, worksheets) vertically, while the right column continues to host the persistent preview panel aligned with the top content. All content sits within a centered max-width container, following a 12-column grid.
layout_strategies.space_allocation: Below the fixed global header (not part of this component list), the dashboard-root area fills the remaining viewport height. The top hero band (tabs + summary + preview) visually occupies roughly 35–45% of the above-the-fold area on a typical laptop screen. Horizontally, the internal grid divides into a 60/40 split: about 7 of 12 columns for the main text/content on the left (tabs, summary stats, and all lists) and about 5 of 12 columns for the preview panel on the right. Generous outer padding (24–32px) and vertical section gaps (48–64px) create ample breathing room, ensuring that even with multiple sections the page still feels airy and premium. Lists and cards don’t stretch edge-to-edge; they sit as white panels on the dark teal background with noticeable margins on all sides.
layout_strategies.content_density: The density mirrors a modern SaaS dashboard with an editorial feel: low to medium density with large headings, clear section titles, and spacious list rows. Each major region (tabs, summary stats block, each section wrapper, and the preview panel) is clearly separated by 32–64px vertical spacing. Within lists, rows have tall click targets with 16–24px vertical padding. There are no sidebars beyond the main right-hand preview panel and no busy secondary controls above the fold, keeping the immediate focus on key stats and core collections.
layout_strategies.visual_flow: The visual flow follows an F/Z-pattern adapted from the mockup. The eye starts at the upper-left of the dashboard with the section tabs, then moves horizontally across the top area past any summary labels and toward the upper-right corner of the preview panel’s header. From there it travels diagonally down to the bold summary stats headlines on the left, then scans right toward the preview content. As users scroll, the pattern repeats: attention returns to the left column for section titles and lists, then sweeps right to the sticky preview panel. Strong contrast between the dark teal background and white cards, plus bold typography in the summary and section headers, anchors the user’s attention on primary information while the preview panel acts as a consistent, secondary focal point.

layout.overall_description: The dashboard sits on a rich dark teal canvas with a centered content column roughly 1140–1280px wide. Just below the global site header, a spacious top band acts as the dashboard ‘hero’: at the top-left, compact section tabs resemble modern pill-shaped toggles; beneath them, a wide summary stats block in the left two-thirds shows key metrics in large, confident typography. To its right, a tall, card-like preview panel occupies the remaining third, aligned so its top edge sits near the mid/upper height of the summary area, echoing the hero image alignment in the mockup. Below this hero band, the left column continues downward, stacking three clearly separated sections: Assessments, Plans, and Worksheets, each presented as a white card-style block containing a heading and a clean list of items. The right-hand preview panel remains vertically aligned and can stay sticky, so as users scroll through lists on the left, the selected item’s details remain visible on the right. Generous vertical spacing, wide margins, and minimal chrome keep the layout calm and premium, while the 60/40 column split and repeated left-to-right reading pattern create a confident, SaaS-like dashboard experience within a life-coaching context.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The dashboard-root occupies the full width of the viewport beneath the fixed global header, extending down to the bottom of the page. Inside it, all content is constrained to a centered container with a max width of approximately 1140–1280px, aligned centrally on large screens and spanning nearly full width on narrow screens. The background of this region is a solid dark teal, with all interactive content presented on lighter or white surface cards sitting on top. Vertical padding of roughly 48–64px is applied at the top and bottom of this container, giving the entire dashboard a spacious, premium frame. The internal layout uses a 12-column grid, with all primary content (tabs, summary stats, sections, preview panel) snapped to this grid and aligned so that the left content column and right preview column share a consistent gutter.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static main page container that scrolls with the page; acts as the parent for all child layout behavior, respecting the fixed global header above it.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The section tabs sit at the very top of the content area within dashboard-root, just below the global header. They are left-aligned within the centered content container, occupying roughly the first 4–6 grid columns on desktop. Visually, they appear as a horizontal row of pill-shaped tabs or segmented controls, each with 8–12px vertical padding and 16–24px horizontal padding, separated by 8–16px gaps. There is a comfortable 16–24px of space above them (to separate from the header) and about 24–32px below them before the summary stats block begins. On smaller screens, the tabs may wrap onto a second line but remain anchored to the left, maintaining the visual role of a compact, high-level mode selector similar to the ‘Blog’ pill in the mockup.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Normal flow element at the top of the dashboard content; optionally can become sticky just below the global header on scroll for easier section switching, but stays flush with the content container.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The summary stats block sits directly below the section tabs, aligned to the left and occupying approximately 60% of the content container width on desktop (around 7 of 12 grid columns). It begins about 24–32px beneath the tabs, forming the main ‘hero text’ of the dashboard. The block itself can be a card or lightly elevated surface with 24–32px internal padding, containing a large, bold heading (e.g., ‘Your Coaching Overview’) and 2–4 key metrics arranged in either a single column or a 2-column layout. The top of this block aligns horizontally with the upper third or mid-area of the preview panel on the right, echoing the hero title and image alignment from the mockup. There is 32–48px of vertical space below the stats block before the first content section begins, ensuring that this hero-like information has room to stand out.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Participates in normal document flow in the left column; height may expand with content but always maintains the same left alignment and margin relative to the preview panel.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The assessments-section appears directly below the summary stats block in the left column, aligned to the same left edge and spanning the same 60% width (approximately 7 of 12 grid columns) on desktop. A section title row (e.g., ‘Saved Assessments’) sits at the top of this block, with 8–16px of space above any internal controls like filters. The entire section is spaced 32–48px below the summary stats block, maintaining the mockup’s generous vertical section separation. The section wrapper acts like a white or light surface card with 24px internal padding and rounded corners, clearly differentiating it from the dark teal background. It extends downward just enough to wrap the list of assessments, with 24–32px of padding at the bottom before the next section begins.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Flows vertically in the left column; on smaller screens it spans nearly 100% of available width and pushes subsequent sections downward as it grows.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The saved-assessments-list is nested inside the assessments-section and fills its width from left to right, minus the section’s internal padding. It begins 16–24px below the section heading. Each list item appears as a row with generous vertical padding (12–16px top and bottom) and clear spacing between rows (8–12px), maintaining a low-density, touch-friendly feel. On desktop, the list visually occupies about 60% of the page width and sits horizontally opposite the right-side preview panel. As the user scrolls through the list, the right preview remains in place, creating a strong left→right interaction pattern: selecting an item on the left updates detail content on the right without changing the overall layout. If the list is long, it simply extends downward within the section, maintaining a consistent left margin and clear separation from the sections below.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Standard vertical list that scrolls with the page; no fixed height by default, though it can be given a max-height with internal scrolling on smaller viewports if needed to prevent the page from feeling too tall.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The assessment-preview-panel occupies the right-hand column of the main content area, beginning near the top of the hero band. On desktop, it spans approximately 40% of the content container width (about 5 of 12 grid columns), with its left edge aligned to a consistent vertical grid line opposite the left content. Vertically, its top aligns with the upper third or mid-line of the summary stats block, mimicking the way the hero image was positioned relative to the heading in the mockup. The panel is a prominent, tall card with 24–32px internal padding, rounded corners, and perhaps a subtle shadow, giving it a distinct, elevated presence on the dark teal background. It extends downward roughly to match or exceed the height of the summary stats block and the assessments section above the fold, ensuring it remains visible as the user begins to scroll. When there is selected content, the panel shows a clear title at the top, followed by key details and maybe a small progress visualization, all arranged with ample spacing. On tablet and smaller screens, this panel collapses beneath the lists, stretching to full width and appearing after the primary sections so that vertical reading remains natural.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: On medium and large screens the panel behaves as a sticky or semi-sticky element: it remains fixed relative to the viewport once its top reaches a certain threshold (just below the global header and tabs), while the left column scrolls. On small screens, it returns to normal flow and appears as a block below the lists.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The plans-section sits below the assessments-section in the same left-hand column, aligned to the same left margin and spanning the same width (around 60% of the content container on desktop). There is a notable 32–48px vertical gap between the bottom of the assessments-section and the top of the plans-section, preserving the calm, editorial rhythm from the mockup. Within this wrapper, a section heading such as ‘Coaching Plans’ is placed at the top, followed by any optional subtext or filters, and then the saved-plans-list. The section wrapper once again resembles a white, rounded card with 24px internal padding, clearly separated visually from the dark teal background.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Standard flowing section that stacks vertically under the assessments section in the left column, adjusting height based on its list length and pushing the worksheets section further down as needed.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The saved-plans-list resides inside the plans-section, taking up the full internal width of the section. Similar to the assessments list, it begins 16–24px below the section title and consists of vertically stacked plan rows with generous vertical padding (12–16px) and 8–12px spacing between entries. On desktop, these items remain within the wider left column and are visually read first before the user glances to the right preview panel. If plan selection is supported, selecting a plan updates the same right-hand preview panel, reinforcing the left-source/right-detail interaction pattern. On smaller screens, the list stretches nearly full width and appears above the preview panel once the layout stacks.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Simple, scrollable list that grows vertically with content; no special behavior beyond standard flow within its section wrapper.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The worksheets-section is placed below the plans-section in the left column, aligning to the same left edge and width. It begins after a generous vertical gap of 32–48px, ensuring each section is clearly distinct. Like the other sections, it appears as a white or light card with 24px padding and a strong section title at the top (e.g., ‘Worksheets & Exercises’). Inside, it contains the saved-worksheets-list. The bottom of this section is padded with 32–48px before reaching the end of the dashboard content, mirroring the comfortable section margins seen at the top of the page.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Flows naturally at the bottom of the left column; its height is determined by the number of worksheet items, and it scrolls with the page while the right-hand preview may remain sticky on large screens.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The saved-worksheets-list fills the interior of the worksheets-section card, starting 16–24px below the section heading. Each worksheet item is displayed as a full-width row with generous padding and clear typographic hierarchy (title, brief description or tag, and possibly a progress indicator). The rows are separated by 8–12px of vertical space to maintain the low-density, premium feel. On desktop, the list is confined to the left column in line with the other lists; selecting an item triggers an update in the persistent preview panel to the right, maintaining a coherent left-list/right-detail pattern across all three content types.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Simple, vertically expanding list that scrolls with the rest of the content; no special fixed behavior, but its selections influence what appears in the assessment-preview-panel.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The empty-state-message appears contextually inside the main content area when a particular list or the overall dashboard has no data to display. When tied to a specific section (e.g., no assessments), it is centered within that section wrapper, replacing the list. The message is presented as a compact card or a centered block of text and illustration inside the white section card, with generous padding (24–32px) and ample whitespace around it. It does not extend to the right column; instead, it remains grounded in the left content area, preserving the overall layout structure. When used at the global level (e.g., no data at all), it can be centered horizontally within the content container, spanning the width of the left column and positioned roughly in the middle of the viewport height, while the right preview panel either hides or shows a related empty detail state.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Appears in place of lists or primary content blocks but follows the same layout boundaries (left column, within section cards). It occupies normal flow space and does not overlay other elements.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The dashboard-loading-indicator appears atop the dashboard-root content when data is being fetched. Visually, it is centered within the main content container, both horizontally and vertically within the visible area, slightly dimming the background content if present. A simple spinner or progress indicator is shown, possibly within a subtle overlay card with 16–24px padding. The indicator does not cover the global header but can lightly veil the tabs, summary stats, sections, and preview panel, keeping the user’s attention in the central area while reinforcing the spacious, minimalist styling.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Temporal overlay within the dashboard-root that sits on top of other components using an absolute/fixed position centered in the viewport; non-interactive background elements remain visible but not usable while it is active.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The dashboard-error-banner is positioned at the top of the dashboard-root content area, directly beneath the section tabs and above the summary stats block. It spans the full width of the content container (across both the left and right columns) but remains inset from the viewport edges, respecting the same horizontal margins as all other content. The banner is a prominent horizontal strip with high contrast (e.g., a saturated error color against the dark teal background), 12–16px vertical padding, and 16–24px horizontal padding, with 16–24px of vertical space separating it from adjacent elements above and below. It is easily scannable in the F-pattern’s top row, making system-level issues immediately visible without disrupting the broader layout.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Standard in-flow element that pushes the summary stats and subsequent content downward while present; it scrolls away naturally with the page and does not remain fixed.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The assessment-preview-error appears inside the assessment-preview-panel when there is a failure loading or rendering the selected detail. It replaces the usual preview content but keeps the panel’s overall size and position in the right column intact. The error is displayed as a centered message (and optional icon) within the panel, with generous padding (24–32px) and breathing room above and below. The card’s framing and elevation remain identical to the normal preview state, ensuring visual continuity. The left column lists and sections remain visible and unaffected, so users can still interact with them while being clearly informed that details for the current selection cannot be shown.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Contained entirely within the existing preview panel card, occupying its normal layout space and scrolling behavior. It does not alter the surrounding right-column structure.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The section-url-sync component operates behind the scenes to keep the currently active section or selection in sync with the URL, but it has no visual representation. Conceptually, it is associated with the same region as the section tabs and the selected states of each list, but it does not take up any space on the page and does not alter the visible layout.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Non-visual, zero-size controller that does not participate in layout or spatial behavior; it simply reacts to and updates state based on URL changes.

name: Resources & Worksheets
title: Resources & Worksheets
description: Browse, filter, and navigate to downloadable worksheets and resources, using topic, audience, and page count filters, with results powered by searchWorksheets.

core_features: Load available filter options (topics, audiences, page count ranges) via getWorksheetFilterOptions and render them in a filter form.
Read optional URL parameters (topic, audience, maxPages, page, sortBy) to initialize the current filter, sorting, and pagination state.
Search and list worksheets/resources via searchWorksheets using the current filter, sorting, and pagination state.
Allow users to adjust filters (topic, audience, maximum page count) and refresh the results, updating the URL to reflect the new state.
Allow users to change result pages (pagination) and sorting, updating URL parameters and re-calling searchWorksheets.
Allow users to open a worksheet detail page by clicking a worksheet/resource item, navigating to worksheet_detail.html with the worksheet ID in the URL.
user_workflows:
Workflow: Initial page load without pre-filters
1) User navigates to resources.html without query parameters.
2) The page controller reads the empty URL state (no topic, audience, or maxPages).
3) The page controller calls getWorksheetFilterOptions to retrieve all available topics, audiences, and page count options.
4) The filter form is initialized with no selection (unfiltered state) using the options returned by getWorksheetFilterOptions.
5) The page controller calls searchWorksheets with an empty filters object (no topic, audience, or maxPages), a default sortBy value, and an initial page and pageSize.
6) The results list displays the returned worksheets and pagination controls based on totalCount.
7) The URL is kept in sync with the current state (e.g., page and sortBy) even if filters are empty.

Workflow: Page load with pre-applied filters from another page
1) User clicks a link from index.html or blog.html that opens resources.html with query parameters topic, audience, and/or maxPages.
2) The page controller parses these URL parameters and sets the initial filter state accordingly.
3) The controller calls getWorksheetFilterOptions and populates the filter form, pre-selecting options that match the URL parameters when available.
4) The controller then calls searchWorksheets with filters constructed from the URL (topic, audience, maxPages), along with a default or URL-provided sortBy and page.
5) The results list shows only worksheets matching the provided filters.
6) The active filter summary component displays the currently applied topic, audience, and maximum page count based on URL state.

Workflow: User applies or changes filters
1) User adjusts one or more controls in the filter form (topic, audience, maximum page count) and submits the filters.
2) The filter form emits an event with the new filter values to the page controller.
3) The page controller updates the URL query parameters (topic, audience, maxPages), resets page to the first page, and maintains/updates sortBy if necessary.
4) The page controller calls searchWorksheets with the updated filters and page=1.
5) The results list and pagination controls update to reflect the new results.
6) The active filter summary updates based on the latest URL state.

Workflow: User clears a single filter or all filters
1) User clicks to remove a specific filter (for example, topic) from the active filter summary or uses a clear/reset button in the filter form.
2) The respective component notifies the page controller with the updated filter set.
3) The page controller removes the corresponding query parameter(s) from the URL (e.g., topic) and, if clearing all, also removes audience and maxPages.
4) Page is reset to the first page, and searchWorksheets is called with the reduced/empty filters object.
5) The results list updates to show the broader or unfiltered set of worksheets, and the filter form reflects cleared selections.

Workflow: Pagination through results
1) User clicks on a pagination control (e.g., next page, previous page, or a specific page number).
2) The pagination component emits an event to the page controller with the new page number.
3) The page controller updates the page query parameter in the URL, leaving topic, audience, maxPages, and sortBy unchanged.
4) The page controller calls searchWorksheets with the current filters and sortBy plus the new page value.
5) The results list updates to show the worksheets for that page, and the pagination component reflects the new current page.

Workflow: Changing sort order
1) User selects a different sort option (for example, ordering by page count or alphabetically) in the sort control.
2) The sort control emits an event with the selected sortBy value to the page controller.
3) The page controller updates the sortBy query parameter and resets the page to the first page.
4) The page controller calls searchWorksheets with the current filters and the new sortBy and page=1.
5) The results list and pagination update according to the new ordering.

Workflow: Navigating to a worksheet detail
1) User clicks on a worksheet/resource item displayed in the results list.
2) The results list component identifies the Worksheet.id associated with the clicked item.
3) The browser navigates to worksheet_detail.html with the id query parameter set to the worksheetId.
4) No additional data is loaded on the current page; the detail page handles its own data loading based on the id parameter.
interactions: Filter selection interaction: When a user selects or changes the topic, audience, or maximum page count in the filter form, the component updates its internal form state and can either apply filters immediately (on change) or wait for an explicit form submission (onSubmit), then notifies the page controller to update URL state and re-run searchWorksheets.

Filter submission interaction: When a user submits the filter form, the form validates that the selected values match available options returned by getWorksheetFilterOptions, then emits an onSubmitFilters event with the selected topic, audience, and maxPages, triggering a URL update and a new searchWorksheets call.

Filter reset interaction: When a user clicks a reset/clear button, the filter form clears its selections and emits an onResetFilters event with empty/null values, prompting the page controller to remove the topic, audience, and maxPages parameters from the URL and to call searchWorksheets with an empty filters object.

Active filter chip interaction: When a user clicks a removal control on an active filter (for example, removing only the audience filter), the active filter summary component emits an onRemoveFilter event specifying which filter key to remove; the page controller updates the URL parameters accordingly and re-calls searchWorksheets.

Pagination interaction: When a user interacts with the pagination controls, the pagination component emits an onPageChange event with the desired page number; the page controller updates the page URL parameter and calls searchWorksheets with existing filters and sortBy, updating the results list.

Sorting interaction: When a user selects a new sort option, the sort control emits an onSortChange event containing the selected sortBy value; the page controller updates the sortBy URL parameter, resets the page parameter, and re-calls searchWorksheets.

Result item click interaction: When a user clicks a worksheet/resource card or row, the results list emits an onWorksheetSelect event with the Worksheet.id; the page then navigates to worksheet_detail.html?id=<Worksheet.id> based on that ID.

Loading and error state interaction: When searchWorksheets or getWorksheetFilterOptions is in progress, the page controller exposes a loading state that can be used by components to disable inputs or show a loading indicator; if an error occurs, the controller exposes an error state that can be displayed in a functional error panel with an option to retry the failed call.
page_functionality.state_logic: The page relies exclusively on URL query parameters to represent and restore state; no localStorage or other client-side persistence is used. On load, the page controller parses the following query parameters: topic (string), audience (string), maxPages (number), page (number), and sortBy (string). For topic, audience, and maxPages, if a parameter is present and non-empty, it is included in the filters object passed to searchWorksheets; if absent, that filter is omitted. The initial page value defaults to the first page if not provided; if provided, it is used as the page parameter for searchWorksheets. The sortBy value is taken from the URL if present; otherwise, a default sortBy value configured by the application is used. Any change to filters, page, or sortBy made via user interaction results in the page controller updating the browser URL query string (adding, changing, or removing parameters) and then re-invoking searchWorksheets with filters derived from the updated URL state. This ensures that page reloads and shared links will always reproduce the same filter, sorting, and pagination state. The filter form and active filter summary do not maintain independent persistent state; they are always derived from the parsed URL state and the options returned by getWorksheetFilterOptions.

components.functionality: Coordinates data loading and state management for the Resources & Worksheets page. On initialization, reads URL query parameters (topic, audience, maxPages, page, sortBy), calls getWorksheetFilterOptions to obtain available filter options, and calls searchWorksheets with filters constructed from URL parameters plus current pagination and sorting. Listens for events from child components (filters, pagination, sorting, selection) to update URL parameters and re-execute searchWorksheets as needed.

Provides controls for selecting topic, audience, and maximum page count for worksheet search. It displays options derived from the response of getWorksheetFilterOptions and initializes its selected values based on the current URL state provided by the page controller. When the user submits the form, it emits the updated filter values for application. It can also provide a reset action to clear all filters.

Displays a concise summary of the currently applied filters (topic, audience, maxPages) based on the URL-derived state provided by the page controller. Each active filter can be individually removed by the user. When a filter is removed, the component notifies the page controller so that the URL state and searchWorksheets call can be updated accordingly.

Allows the user to specify how worksheet results should be ordered (for example, by relevance or page count). It reflects the current sortBy state derived from the URL and emits events when the user selects a different sort option, enabling the page controller to update URL parameters and re-call searchWorksheets.

Renders the list of worksheets/resources returned by searchWorksheets. Each item displays key Worksheet fields such as title, description, topic, audience, page_count, and format. Clicking an item triggers navigation to worksheet_detail.html using the Worksheet.id. The component also displays loading or empty states based on flags and counts provided by the page controller.

Displays pagination based on the totalCount value from searchWorksheets and the current page state from the URL. It calculates the available pages using totalCount and the configured pageSize and allows the user to navigate between pages. On interaction, it emits an onPageChange event so the page controller can update URL parameters and re-call searchWorksheets.

Shows global loading and error feedback for both getWorksheetFilterOptions and searchWorksheets calls. When loading is true, it can disable filter controls and indicate that results are being fetched. When an error state is present, it displays an error message and offers a retry action that triggers the page controller to re-run the failed request.

layout.grid_system: Implied 12-column responsive grid with a centered maximum content width (likely 1140–1280px). Hero content appears to span about 6–7 columns for text and 5–6 columns for the image.
layout.layout_pattern: Below the fixed full-width site header, the Resources & Worksheets page begins with a full-width hero band managed by the page controller. This hero mirrors the blog mockup: a left-aligned content column (category pill, large page title, short description) and a right-aligned visual column (illustrative resource/worksheet image or abstract card stack). Below the hero, the main content flows linearly in a single centered content column: first the worksheet filter form, then an active filter summary bar paired with the sort control as a shared toolbar row, followed by the worksheet results list, and finally the pagination controls at the bottom. The loading/error panel occupies the same vertical space as the results list section when active, temporarily replacing or overlaying the cards.

layout_strategies.content_arrangement: Below the fixed full-width site header, the Resources & Worksheets page begins with a full-width hero band managed by the page controller. This hero mirrors the blog mockup: a left-aligned content column (category pill, large page title, short description) and a right-aligned visual column (illustrative resource/worksheet image or abstract card stack). Below the hero, the main content flows linearly in a single centered content column: first the worksheet filter form, then an active filter summary bar paired with the sort control as a shared toolbar row, followed by the worksheet results list, and finally the pagination controls at the bottom. The loading/error panel occupies the same vertical space as the results list section when active, temporarily replacing or overlaying the cards.
layout_strategies.space_allocation: The header remains a slim band (~10–15% of viewport height). The hero controlled by the page controller takes up the majority of the above-the-fold area (~45–60% of viewport height), using a 12-column grid: roughly 55–60% width for the left text column and 40–45% for the right visual column within a centered max-width container (about 1140–1280px). Below the hero, the filters and results sit on a white or very light panel that spans the full content width but not the full viewport, leaving wide teal margins at the sides. Vertical spacing between major blocks (hero → filters, filters → toolbar, toolbar → results, results → pagination) follows a 48–64px rhythm, with generous internal padding (24–32px) inside cards and panels. The results list itself occupies most of the remaining vertical space, with pagination taking a relatively small band at the bottom.
layout_strategies.content_density: Density remains low and editorial: each section has clear breathing room above and below, and individual interactive components (filter fields, sort dropdown, result cards, pagination buttons) have ample padding and spacing based on the 8px scale. Typography stays large and confident, especially for the hero title and resource names. The filter form is spaciously arranged in one or two rows instead of tightly packed, the active filter summary and sort control share a light-weight toolbar with plenty of whitespace, and the results list cards have generous padding and separation, emphasizing clarity over quantity. No sidebars or secondary distractions are introduced; everything is arranged in a straightforward vertical flow.
layout_strategies.visual_flow: The visual flow mirrors the original F/Z pattern: the user’s eye starts at the global logo and navigation in the top-left, moves across to header actions, then diagonally down to the category pill and large 'Resources & Worksheets' heading in the hero’s left column. From there, the gaze travels horizontally to the right hero visual. As the user scrolls, the eye drops down to the filter form at the left of the main content column, continues through the active filter summary on the left side of the toolbar, then glances to the right-aligned sort control. Finally, attention moves down through the vertically stacked worksheet cards and ends on the centered pagination controls. When the status panel is visible, it interrupts this flow intentionally by becoming the primary focal element in the results region.

layout.overall_description: The page sits on a dark teal canvas with a centered content column. A slim, fixed header with logo, navigation, and a right-aligned primary action anchors the top. Directly beneath, a generous hero section stretches full width: on the left, a small 'Resources' or 'Worksheets' pill, a bold multi-line page title, and a short explanatory paragraph occupy roughly 60% of the content width, all left-aligned with ample line height. On the right, taking the remaining 40%, a tall rectangular visual panel showcases an illustrative worksheet preview, stack of pages, or abstract coaching imagery, aligned vertically with the midpoint of the title. The hero fades into the main content area below, where a light surface sits within the same centered grid. The filter form appears first as a full-width, softly elevated panel with neatly arranged dropdowns and inputs. Just below, a slim toolbar row runs across: active filter chips and a result count on the left, a sort dropdown aligned to the right. Beneath this, the worksheet results unfold in two columns of roomy cards on desktop (collapsing to one column on smaller screens), each card separated by comfortable gaps. At the bottom, centered pagination controls provide calm, pill-shaped navigation with generous horizontal padding. Whenever loading or errors occur, a dedicated status panel appears in the same region as the results, centered and clearly legible, briefly becoming the main focal point while still respecting the overall spacious, modern SaaS aesthetic tailored to life coaching content.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The resources-page-controller occupies the entire page area below the fixed global header, extending full-width across the viewport with a dark teal background. It defines a centered content container (max-width ~1140–1280px) that all child components align to. At the top of this container, it renders the hero band: a two-column layout using the implied 12-column grid, where the left column spans about 7/12 of the width and the right column spans about 5/12. The left column is anchored to the left edge of the content container and contains a small category pill ('Resources', 'Worksheets', or 'Blog'), positioned near the top, followed by a large, multi-line page heading ('Resources & Worksheets') and a short one- or two-line description. Generous vertical spacing separates these elements (16–24px between pill and title, 16–24px between title and description). The right column is aligned to the right edge of the content container and holds a tall rectangular visual placeholder or hero illustration, vertically aligned so its top sits roughly in line with the midpoint between the page title's first and second lines, echoing the mockup. Surrounding the hero content is ample negative space: at least 64px top margin from the global header, and 64px bottom spacing before the main content (filters and results) begins. Below the hero, the controller provides a subtle transition (e.g., the main area can sit on a slightly lighter panel or card) and vertically stacks: the worksheet-filter-form, then the active-filter-summary and worksheet-sort-control (sharing a toolbar row), followed by the worksheet-results-list, and finally the worksheet-pagination-controls. Horizontally, all of these components are centered within the same content container and left-aligned within their own surfaces, maintaining a clean, editorial column.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static, full-width, acts as the main layout scaffold. It scrolls with the page, with the hero portion effectively occupying the initial above-the-fold viewport area on most devices.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The worksheet-filter-form sits immediately below the hero section within the same centered content container, separated from the hero by a generous vertical gap of about 48–64px. It appears as a wide, horizontally oriented card or panel that spans nearly the full width of the content container (around 90–100% of the container width), with rounded corners and a light background to contrast against the dark teal canvas. Internally, the form has substantial padding (24–32px on all sides), making each field feel roomy. On desktop, filter controls (topic, audience, page count, and any additional fields) are arranged in one or two rows: each control occupies roughly 2–3 grid columns, creating a balanced, left-to-right reading flow across the panel. Related controls are grouped together with 16–24px gaps horizontally and vertically. The left edge of the first filter aligns with the left edge of the hero text above, reinforcing the vertical column. On narrower viewports, the form collapses to a single column, stacking fields vertically with consistent 16–24px gaps, still spanning most of the content width. There is generous whitespace above and below the lowest row of fields to maintain the low-density, premium feel.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static within the main scroll; remains at the top of the main content section so that after a small scroll, it stays near the top of the viewport, making filter adjustments easy without feeling sticky or fixed.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The active-filter-summary is positioned directly below the worksheet-filter-form, separated by a modest vertical gap of about 24–32px to feel connected yet distinct. It is part of a shared horizontal toolbar row that spans the full width of the content container. Within this row, the summary area occupies the left side, taking roughly 60–70% of the container width. It is left-aligned, starting at the same vertical column as the hero heading and filter form. The summary appears as a slim bar with minimal height: a short label ('Active filters' or dynamic result count) followed by pill-shaped chips representing each active filter. The chips align in a horizontal line, wrapping to a second line if needed, but maintaining 8–12px spacing between chips so the bar never feels crowded. This section maintains plenty of negative space above and below the chip row (8–12px padding) so the toolbar looks airy and consistent with the overall aesthetic. On smaller screens, the summary stacks first in the toolbar, with the sort control (described below) dropping beneath it.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static within its toolbar row. It shares horizontal space with the sort control and responds responsively by allowing chips to wrap and by yielding vertical space on small screens (stacked above the sort control).

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The worksheet-sort-control shares the same horizontal toolbar row as the active-filter-summary, aligned to the far right edge of the content container. On desktop, the toolbar appears as a single row: active-filter-summary occupying the left ~60–70%, and the sort control occupying the right ~30–40%, with clear separation but on the same baseline. The sort control itself is a compact, pill-like dropdown or segmented control, right-aligned, with 8–12px vertical padding and 16–24px horizontal padding inside the control. It sits flush with the right grid line of the content container, visually counterbalancing the left-aligned summary and guiding the eye across in a light F-pattern. Around the toolbar row, there is 16–24px vertical spacing before the results list begins, ensuring this control doesn’t feel cramped. On tablet and mobile, this component collapses below the active-filter-summary, aligning left instead of right, but still maintaining a small horizontal offset to distinguish it (e.g., a slight indent or smaller max-width) so it reads as a separate control while remaining in the same visual group.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static; sits within the scrolling toolbar region. It maintains right alignment on larger screens and shifts to left-aligned, stacked layout below the active filter summary on smaller breakpoints.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The worksheet-results-list sits directly beneath the toolbar row (active-filter-summary + worksheet-sort-control) with a comfortable vertical gap of about 24–32px. It occupies the full width of the content container but leaves generous gutters on the left and right relative to the viewport. On desktop, the results are presented as a grid of spacious cards: typically two columns across, each card spanning about 5–5.5 columns with a 1–2 column gutter between them. Cards are aligned to the same left and right grid lines that define the hero and filter form, creating a strong structural rhythm. Each card has ample internal padding (24–32px), a clearly prominent worksheet title, small metadata (topic, audience, page count) aligned below, and possibly a subtle border or shadow to lift it from the background. Vertically, there is 24–32px spacing between card rows, keeping the density low and scannability high. On large monitors, the list can expand vertically while maintaining the two-column constraint to preserve the editorial feel. On tablet and mobile, the layout gracefully collapses to a single column, with each card spanning 100% of the content width and maintaining the same inner padding and vertical spacing. The top edge of the first card aligns visually with the left column from the hero and filters, anchoring the page’s vertical flow.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static and scrollable with the page. The list defines the main scrollable body of the page; its height varies with the number of results, and it yields space to the status panel when loading or error states are active (the status panel temporarily replaces the visible cards).

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The worksheet-pagination-controls are positioned at the bottom of the results section, centered horizontally within the content container and separated from the last row of cards by a generous 32–48px vertical gap. This spacing visually concludes the list and creates a distinct navigation band. The pagination UI is compact but airy: a central row of page pills or numbers (e.g., 'Previous', page numbers, 'Next'), each with 8–12px vertical padding and 16–24px horizontal padding, separated by 8–12px gaps. On desktop, the controls align to the exact center of the content container, echoing the calm, balanced SaaS aesthetic. On smaller screens, the controls remain centered but may show fewer visible page numbers to avoid crowding, still using the same padding scale. Additional vertical space (32–48px) below the controls ensures that the bottom of the page feels open and doesn’t abruptly cut off content, supporting the overall spacious design.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static, appearing after the results list within the normal scroll flow. It remains at the bottom of the current results page; as users paginate, the results list above it updates while the pagination controls themselves stay in the same spatial position relative to the bottom of the viewport after scrolling.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The worksheet-loading-and-error-panel occupies the same horizontal position as the worksheet-results-list, aligned within the content container under the toolbar row. When active, it either replaces the visible cards or overlays them with a semi-opaque layer, depending on the implementation. In either case, it is visually centered: the panel’s main content (spinner, icon, message) is horizontally centered and sits roughly mid-height within the vertical space where the results would normally appear, creating a clear focal point. The panel spans a moderate width—typically about 60–70% of the content container on desktop—so it feels substantial but not overpowering, with plenty of teal or light background space surrounding it. Internal padding is generous (32–40px), and text is set in a readable, confident size with ample line height, matching the calm, professional tone. For errors, a subtle accent color or icon can be introduced, but the panel remains minimalist and free of clutter. On smaller screens, the panel expands to nearly 100% of the content width while maintaining the same padded, centered appearance, pushing pagination out of sight if necessary so the user focuses on the message and recommended actions.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Contextual and conditional. When active, it effectively takes over the results region, either by displacing the worksheet-results-list in the layout or by overlaying it with a centered message. It scrolls with the page, but its content is vertically centered within the results area to draw immediate attention.

name: Worksheet Detail
title: Worksheet Detail
description: Displays details and download options for a single worksheet identified by a URL parameter and allows saving it to the user's Dashboard toolkit.

core_features: Read the worksheet ID from the URL parameter "id" and load worksheet data using getWorksheetDetail(worksheetId).
Display worksheet metadata including title, description, topic, audience, page count, format, and availability state.
Provide a primary action to download or view the worksheet using the worksheet.download_url field when available.
Allow the user to save the worksheet to their personal toolkit/Dashboard using saveWorksheet(worksheetId, source).
Reflect and persist the saved state returned from getWorksheetDetail and saveWorksheet, updating UI controls accordingly.
Provide navigation back to the resources listing (resources.html) and forward to the Dashboard worksheet section (dashboard.html?section=worksheets) after saving.
user_workflows: 1) Open Worksheet Detail from Resources or Dashboard: User clicks a worksheet item on resources.html, index.html, or dashboard.html, which links to worksheet_detail.html?id=<worksheetId>. 2) Page reads the "id" parameter and calls getWorksheetDetail with worksheetId = id. 3) While loading, the page shows a loading state. 4) On success, the page displays worksheet details, download action, and save status. 5) The user can download the worksheet and/or save it to the Dashboard.

1) Save worksheet to Dashboard from the detail page: User lands on worksheet_detail.html?id=<worksheetId>. 2) If getWorksheetDetail returns isSaved = false, the page shows an active "Save to toolkit" button. 3) User clicks "Save to toolkit"; the page calls saveWorksheet(worksheetId = id, source = "worksheet_detail"). 4) While the call is pending, the button shows a saving state and is disabled to prevent duplicates. 5) On success, the page updates local isSaved state to true, uses the returned SavedWorksheet for any internal references, and shows a confirmation message plus an enabled "View in Dashboard" link. 6) On failure, the page shows an error message and re-enables the save button.

1) View saved worksheet in Dashboard: After saving, user clicks "View in Dashboard". 2) Browser navigates to dashboard.html with URL parameter section=worksheets. 3) Dashboard page uses its own logic to highlight or list saved worksheets; this page's responsibility ends at navigation.

1) Return to resources listing: User clicks a "Back to resources" control. 2) Browser navigates to resources.html without additional parameters (or with only those defined by that page). 3) User continues browsing other worksheets or resources.

1) Handle missing or invalid worksheet ID: User opens worksheet_detail.html without an "id" parameter or with an invalid ID. 2) Page-init logic detects missing/invalid id and either avoids calling getWorksheetDetail or handles an error response. 3) Page shows an appropriate error state with an option to navigate back to resources.html and does not show download/save actions.
interactions: On page load, read URL parameter "id"; if present, trigger getWorksheetDetail(worksheetId = id); if absent, set an error state and show a navigation option back to resources.html.
On successful getWorksheetDetail response, bind the returned worksheet fields to detail components and set the saved state flag from isSaved.

On getWorksheetDetail failure (for example, not found or network error), show an error message with a retry action that re-calls getWorksheetDetail using the current "id" parameter and a separate action to return to resources.html.

When the user clicks the primary download/view button, open the worksheet.download_url in the same or a new browser context based on standard link behavior; if download_url is missing or empty, disable the control and show a descriptive message instead.

When the user clicks the "Save to toolkit" button (visible only if isSaved is false), call saveWorksheet with worksheetId equal to the current URL "id" and source set to "worksheet_detail". While pending, disable the button and optionally show a spinner state.

On successful saveWorksheet response, update the local saved state to true, store the returned SavedWorksheet object in page state for any subsequent logic, update the button label/state to indicate the worksheet is saved, and reveal or enable the "View in Dashboard" navigation control.
On saveWorksheet failure, show an error message area linked to the save action and re-enable the "Save to toolkit" button so the user can try again.
When the user clicks "View in Dashboard", navigate to dashboard.html with URL parameter section=worksheets.
When the user clicks "Back to resources", navigate to resources.html without adding worksheet-specific URL parameters.

If the user changes the worksheet ID in the URL (for example via navigation or link click), the page should treat it as a fresh load: clear previous worksheet-specific state, set loading state, and call getWorksheetDetail with the new worksheetId.
page_functionality.state_logic: The page state is driven entirely by URL parameters and API responses, without using localStorage. The primary URL parameter is "id", which must contain the worksheet ID. On initialization, the page reads window.location or equivalent routing state to obtain the "id" value. If "id" is missing, the page enters an invalid-state mode where it does not call getWorksheetDetail and instead shows a message with a link back to resources.html. If "id" is present, the page calls getWorksheetDetail(worksheetId = id). The response provides worksheet (Worksheet entity) and isSaved (boolean). These values populate a page-level state object containing: loading flag, error flag/message, worksheet data, isSaved flag, save-in-progress flag, and last save error. The isSaved flag from getWorksheetDetail determines the initial UI state of the save controls: if true, the save button is disabled or converted to a non-actionable 'Saved' indicator and the 'View in Dashboard' link is available; if false, the primary call-to-action is 'Save to toolkit'. When the user clicks 'Save to toolkit', the page calls saveWorksheet(worksheetId = id, source = 'worksheet_detail'). On success, the page updates isSaved to true and stores the returned SavedWorksheet object in state; on failure, it keeps isSaved unchanged and stores an error in state. Navigation out of the page uses URL-based routing: 'View in Dashboard' links to dashboard.html?section=worksheets, and 'Back to resources' links to resources.html. If the user navigates to worksheet_detail.html with a different id, either via in-site links or manual entry, the page re-runs the initialization sequence for the new worksheetId and discards the previous worksheet-specific state.

components.functionality: Coordinates initial data loading based on the URL parameter, manages loading and error states, holds worksheet and saved state, and exposes actions for saving and reloading.
Displays the main information about the worksheet returned by getWorksheetDetail, such as title, description, topic, audience, page count, and format, without handling any data fetching itself.

Provides a primary download or view action for the worksheet based on the worksheet.download_url field. If the URL is available, renders an enabled control that navigates to that URL; otherwise, renders a disabled state or informational message.

Controls saving the worksheet to the user's toolkit/Dashboard and provides navigation to the Dashboard after saving. It shows a 'Save to toolkit' button if the worksheet is not yet saved, and shows a 'Saved' indicator plus a 'View in Dashboard' navigation control when isSaved is true.

Shows contextual status information for the page, including loading indicators while getWorksheetDetail is in progress, error messages if data loading or saving fails, and success confirmation when a worksheet has been successfully saved.

Provides navigation links to leave the Worksheet Detail page, including 'Back to resources' (resources.html) and, when isSaved is true, 'View in Dashboard' (dashboard.html?section=worksheets). It does not modify worksheet data; it only changes the browser location.

Validates presence of the URL parameter "id" before attempting any API calls. If the parameter is missing or empty, prevents getWorksheetDetail from being called, sets an error state, and exposes navigation back to resources.html.

layout.grid_system: Implied 12-column responsive grid with a centered maximum content width (likely 1140–1280px). Hero content appears to span about 6–7 columns for text and 5–6 columns for the image.
layout.layout_pattern: The page follows a linear, top-to-bottom flow: a full-width fixed header (global logo, nav, primary CTA) sits at the very top. Immediately beneath, the main worksheet detail area behaves like a hero section, contained within a centered max-width wrapper. At the top of this hero area: optional status messages, then a slim navigation row (back link and previous/next). Below that, the hero splits into two columns: the left column holds the worksheet summary and save actions; the right column holds a large worksheet preview with download actions directly associated to it. All content stays left-aligned within the centered container, mirroring the mockup’s text-left / image-right pattern.

layout_strategies.content_arrangement: The page follows a linear, top-to-bottom flow: a full-width fixed header (global logo, nav, primary CTA) sits at the very top. Immediately beneath, the main worksheet detail area behaves like a hero section, contained within a centered max-width wrapper. At the top of this hero area: optional status messages, then a slim navigation row (back link and previous/next). Below that, the hero splits into two columns: the left column holds the worksheet summary and save actions; the right column holds a large worksheet preview with download actions directly associated to it. All content stays left-aligned within the centered container, mirroring the mockup’s text-left / image-right pattern.
layout_strategies.space_allocation: The header occupies a shallow band at the top (~10–15% of viewport height). The worksheet hero area fills most of the above-the-fold space. Within the hero, the content container spans about 80–90% of the viewport width up to a max width of roughly 1140–1280px, centered horizontally. Inside this container, the left column uses about 55–60% of the width for text (summary and save actions), and the right column uses about 40–45% for the visual preview and download actions. Generous outer margins and vertical spacing (48–64px) separate header, status/navigation row, and the hero columns, leaving visible dark teal background padding around the central content.
layout_strategies.content_density: The layout keeps density deliberately low, with only essential elements visible above the fold: status (when present), lightweight navigation, worksheet category pill, title, short description, key actions, and a single prominent preview image. Typographic elements use large headings and open line spacing. Vertical gaps between rows (status, navigation, summary, actions, image/preview) follow 24–48px increments, and internal padding in blocks (status bar, card-like preview, action panels) follows 16–32px, creating a calm, premium SaaS feel rather than a crowded content page.
layout_strategies.visual_flow: The visual flow preserves the mockup’s F/Z pattern. The eye starts at the global header’s logo (top-left), moves across the navigation to any global CTA on the top-right. From there it drops slightly into the worksheet area, first catching any status message, then the top row of worksheet navigation controls (back link left, next/previous right). The gaze then moves diagonally down to the left column: a small category pill at the top, followed immediately by a large, bold worksheet title and concise summary copy. After anchoring on this left column, the eye travels horizontally to the right column, where a large, high-contrast worksheet preview card and prominent download buttons sit. This reinforces the text-first, then-image-and-action flow found in the original hero.
layout_strategies.overall_layout_description: The Worksheet Detail page appears as a focused, SaaS-like hero screen set against a dark teal background. A slim, fixed global header runs along the top, while the main content is centered in a wide but clearly bounded container beneath it. At the top of this container, an optional message bar spans the width, followed by a light navigation row that lets users go back or move between worksheets without visual heaviness. Beneath this, the layout cleanly splits into two columns: on the left, a compact category pill labeled as a worksheet leads into a large, bold title and short, readable description, with a subtle save-to-dashboard control seated just below, all framed by generous negative space. On the right, aligned with the vertical midpoint of the heading, a tall rectangular preview of the worksheet draws attention without overpowering the text, with the primary download buttons arranged directly below it as a tidy action panel. The result is a modern, editorial-style hero that clearly highlights what the worksheet is, while making it immediately obvious how to download it or save it to the user’s toolkit.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The worksheet-detail-controller acts as the invisible structural wrapper for the entire worksheet detail experience. It occupies the full viewport width beneath the fixed global header and sets up a centered content container. This container is horizontally centered and capped at approximately 1140–1280px width, with equal left and right margins so that the inner content feels anchored in the middle of the screen. Vertically, it provides about 64px of spacing below the header before any worksheet-specific elements appear, and another 64px or more before the page’s next major section (if any) below the hero. Inside this wrapper, it establishes the two-column grid: on wider screens, the left column spans roughly 55–60% of the container width and the right column about 40–45%, with a 32px–40px gutter between them. On smaller screens, it stacks these columns vertically, with the left/text column appearing on top and the right/preview column below, maintaining consistent 24–32px vertical gaps between blocks.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static container behavior. Stays positioned under the fixed header, maintaining the centered max-width layout and managing the responsive switch from two-column to single-column stacking as the viewport narrows.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The worksheet-summary occupies the upper portion of the left column within the hero area. It begins roughly 64px below the global header (or status bar if present) and sits directly beneath the worksheet navigation controls, with about 24–32px of vertical space separating them. Horizontally, it aligns to the left edge of the content container and spans approximately 55–60% of the container width. At its very top, a compact category pill (e.g., “Worksheet”) is placed flush left with about 4–8px vertical padding inside and 12–16px horizontal padding, leaving plenty of breathing room above and around it. Directly below this pill, separated by about 16px, a large multi-line heading displays the worksheet title in bold, white or very light text for strong contrast against the dark teal background. The heading can comfortably take up multiple lines without feeling cramped, thanks to generous line height and 24–32px padding beneath. Under the title, a short description or key metadata is displayed as a smaller paragraph block, with 16–24px of spacing from the title, occupying roughly 70–80% of the left column’s width to keep the ragged right edge soft and editorial. The entire summary block is framed with 32–48px of vertical space before the save actions below and the right-column preview to its side, anchoring it as the primary textual focal point.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static within the left column. On large screens it stays aligned to the top of the hero left column, forming the main anchor point of the page. On smaller screens, it becomes the first block in a vertical stack, ensuring the title and description are always seen before preview and download actions.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The worksheet-download-actions panel is located in the right column of the hero, visually associated with the worksheet preview. Within the right column (which occupies about 40–45% of the container width), a tall rectangular preview card of the worksheet sits centered horizontally, aligned so its top roughly matches the vertical mid to upper area of the left column’s heading. The download actions sit directly beneath this preview card, separated by a 24–32px gap. The panel itself spans nearly the full width of the right column, with 16–24px internal padding on all sides, giving it a compact but confident appearance. Primary download buttons (e.g., “Download PDF”) appear as prominent, horizontally oriented buttons aligned left within the panel, with enough spacing (16–24px) between multiple options if more than one format is offered. The panel’s height is modest, no more than a third of the right column’s height, ensuring it feels like a tight, purposeful control area rather than a large block of content. Surrounding the preview-plus-actions group is ample vertical whitespace (32–48px above and below), maintaining the low-density feel while making the download controls easy to find at a glance.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static within the right column, visually anchored to the bottom edge of the preview. On smaller screens where columns stack, the preview appears first and this actions panel sits immediately below it, still left-aligned and spanning most of the available width.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The worksheet-save-actions panel is positioned on the left column, directly associated with the worksheet summary content. It appears immediately below the worksheet description/metadata block, separated by about 24–32px of vertical space so it reads as the next logical step after understanding what the worksheet is. Horizontally, it aligns with the left edge of the heading and occupies roughly 70–80% of the left column’s width, leaving a comfortable margin on the right for visual lightness. The panel is compact, with 8–12px of vertical padding and 16–24px of horizontal padding, and contains one or two key save-related controls (e.g., a primary or secondary button labeled “Save to My Toolkit” and possibly an icon or toggle). These actions are left-aligned and either arranged inline horizontally with 16px gaps or stacked vertically if space is tight, ensuring the primary save control is visually clear. Vertically, there is additional spacing (32–40px) beneath this panel before reaching the bottom of the hero, reinforcing that this is an important but secondary action block beneath the main summary.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static within the left column, locked to the summary block. On smaller screens, it remains directly below the title and description in the vertical stack, ensuring the user sees the save option soon after reading about the worksheet, but before scrolling to the preview and download actions.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The worksheet-status-messages component appears as a horizontal status bar located at the very top of the main content container, just below the fixed global header. It spans nearly the full width of the centered container (up to 1140–1280px) but respects the same side margins as the rest of the content, so its edges line up cleanly with the columns beneath. The bar has a low-to-medium height, with 8–12px vertical padding and 16–24px horizontal padding, enough to make messages legible without feeling bulky. There is about 24–32px of vertical space beneath this status bar before the worksheet-navigation-controls row, keeping it visually separated and easy to scan. The status text is left-aligned, and any accompanying icon sits to the left with a small 8px gap. When no status is present, this bar collapses entirely so there is no visual placeholder, and the navigation row shifts upward to occupy this space.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Appears and disappears dynamically at the top of the main content container, pushing subsequent content down when visible. It remains static in position relative to the container (not sticky), and always sits below the fixed global header.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The worksheet-navigation-controls form a slim horizontal row directly beneath the status messages (if present) and above the two-column hero content. This row spans the same width as the main content container and sits about 24–32px below the status bar or 64px below the global header when no status is shown. Within this row, a subtle “Back to Worksheets” link or breadcrumb is left-aligned near the container’s left edge, functioning as a low-key textual control. On the right side of the same row, previous/next worksheet controls or a simple pager are right-aligned, balancing the left-side back link and creating a light horizontal bar. The height of this row is modest, with 8–12px vertical padding, and 16–24px of space separates it from the top of the worksheet-summary below. The overall effect is that this controls row reads as a light, secondary navigation band that the eye encounters before dropping into the main title and content, maintaining a clean F/Z flow without adding clutter.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Static within the content container, always positioned immediately above the main hero columns. On smaller screens, the layout of controls may stack or center, but the row remains the first worksheet-specific element after the header/status, giving consistent contextual orientation.

component_layouts.layout_narrative: The worksheet-id-guard occupies the same spatial footprint as the main worksheet hero area but conditionally replaces its contents when the worksheet ID is invalid, missing, or still loading. When active, it sits inside the worksheet-detail-controller’s centered container, below the header and any global elements, stretching across the full width of the container with generous vertical padding (48–64px) to feel calm and clear. In a loading state, a centered spinner or progress indicator appears roughly in the middle of the visible hero area, with a short line of explanatory text underneath, both horizontally and vertically centered within this guarded space. In an error state (e.g., worksheet not found), a card-like message block is centered horizontally in the container and vertically positioned around the upper-middle of the viewport, with 24–32px internal padding and a concise error title and message. No other worksheet components (summary, preview, actions, navigation) are visible when this guard is active, preserving the clean, low-density aesthetic and avoiding partial or confusing UI states.
component_layouts.spatial_behavior: Conditionally overlays or replaces the main worksheet content area within the page controller. When active, it suppresses the rendering of the regular hero columns and action panels, effectively becoming the primary content in that region. It remains static in position (non-sticky), centered within the same max-width container as the normal layout.

name: Custom Plan Builder
title: Custom Plan Builder
description: Build, edit, and review a custom coaching plan by combining individual sessions and group workshops, apply price filters and planning constraints, and view or load saved plans.

core_features: Load available individual session offerings and group workshops, plus pricing guidance, using getCustomPlanBuilderOptions.
Filter individual sessions by criteria such as maximum price per session using searchIndividualSessions, with filters reflected in the URL where appropriate.
Filter group workshops by criteria such as maximum workshop price using searchGroupWorkshops, with filters reflected in the URL where appropriate.
Allow users to add or remove individual sessions and group workshops to or from a working custom plan and maintain an in-page running total cost.

Allow users to define plan constraints (for example, required counts of individual sessions and workshops and a maximum total cost) and continuously validate the working plan against those constraints.
